idea.topic: Travel time to Pluto at various speeds
facts: Pluto is an average of 5.9 billion km (39.5 AU) from Earth.
Walking at 5 km/h, it would take you over 134,700 years to reach Pluto!
NASA's New Horizons spacecraft, our fastest visitor, took 9.5 years.
But at light speed (299,792 km/s), the journey is just 5 hours, 28 minutes.
idea.payoff: Even our solar system's dwarf planet reveals the mind-boggling scale of space, making light speed feel like a cheat code for cosmic travel!
idea.title: Pluto: How Long To Get There?! 🚶‍♀️🚀

scenes.visual: A stylized Earth and a small, distant Pluto. A large question mark hovers between them.

scenes.text: Pluto is 5.9 BILLION km away (39.5 AU)
scenes.visual: A graphic showing Earth on one side, Pluto on the other, with a vast, animated dotted line representing the distance. The numbers '5.9 BILLION km' and '39.5 AU' appear sequentially.
A small stick figure walks slowly across the bottom of the screen, with a ridiculously long timeline stretching into the distance. The '134,700+ YEARS!' text animates dramatically.

scenes.text: New Horizons (9.5 yrs) vs. Light Speed (5h 28m!)
scenes.visual: Two distinct lines/paths emerging from Earth to Pluto: one showing a slower, meandering path with a New Horizons icon (9.5 yrs), the other a straight, bright, fast beam of light (5h 28m!). The text compares them.

scenes.text: Space is VAST. Light speed: the ultimate cheat code!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, sweeping view of a nebula or a galaxy, emphasizing the immense scale of space. Earth and Pluto become almost imperceptible in the grand scheme.

youtube.title: Pluto: How Long To Get There?! 🚶‍♀️🚀
